title: phase4c Node binding: napi-rs error-code via JS wrapper, async off-thread via tokio spawn_blocking, close() must drop the handle (GC won't)
content:
phase4c built crates/veclite-node (napi-rs 2.x, workspace-excluded like veclite-py). Key findings:

1. napi-rs 2 CANNOT set a custom JS error `.code` — a thrown napi::Error sets `.code` to the fixed Status enum string ("GenericFailure") and `.message` to the reason. Solution: native encodes `format!("{CODE}\u{1}{message}")` in the reason; a HAND-WRITTEN veclite.js loader (package `main`) requires napi's generated index.js, splits on U+0001, and re-throws a `VecLiteError extends Error` with `.code`. It monkey-patches Database/Collection prototype methods to catch BOTH sync throws and async promise rejections (`out.then===function → out.then(undefined, e=>throw convert(e))`). Committed veclite.js + veclite.d.ts (`export * from './index'` + VecLiteError); index.js/index.d.ts are napi-generated but committed (the *.node binary is gitignored).

2. Async-by-default without blocking the event loop: napi async fn returns a Promise; inside, `tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || blocking_veclite_call()).await` runs the sync VecLite call on tokio's blocking pool. Needs napi features ["napi4","tokio_rt","serde-json"] AND an explicit tokio dep with ["rt","rt-multi-thread"]. VecLite/Collection are Arc-clone Send+Sync so they move into the closure cheaply. Event-loop-liveness test: a 5ms setInterval keeps ticking through a 4k async upsertBatch (max gap <250ms) — proves it's off-thread.

3. Float32Array: sync path uses `arr.as_ref()` zero-copy; ASYNC must copy to Vec<f32> BEFORE spawn_blocking (JS heap can't be touched off-thread) — one unavoidable copy, documented. Hit vectors out: `Float32Array::new(vec)` copies once; true zero-copy-out needs external-buffer + finalizer → follow-up.

4. close() / NODE-013: the advisory file lock releases only when the LAST Arc<Persistence> drops — and JS GC is nondeterministic, so a checkpoint-only close() leaves the lock held → reopen fails LOCKED. Fix: Database holds `Mutex<Option<VecLite>>`; close() `take()`s it and drops OUTSIDE the lock (drop runs the blocking close-checkpoint). ALL methods go through `handle()` → Closed error if None. CAVEAT: a still-referenced napi Collection keeps Persistence alive, so in-process reopen-after-close still races; test cross-process persistence with a child-process writer (execFileSync + `--input-type=module -e`, import via new URL('../veclite.js', import.meta.url).href — a raw Windows path is ERR_UNSUPPORTED_ESM_URL_SCHEME).

5. serde-json feature lets payloads/filters/sparse cross as serde_json::Value params directly (Filter::from_json, serde_json::from_value for SparseVector). #[napi(object)] structs for options give clean generated .d.ts. tsc --strict on the quickstart against the shipped d.ts = acceptance 5 (passed).

Split to phase4i_node-prebuilds-conformance: prebuild matrix + @veclite/* platform packages, Bun/Node-matrix CI, shared conformance runner, external-buffer zero-copy-out, @veclite/onnx.
